playbooks.title: Opportunity Scan
playbooks.selection_hint: First step of validation. Surfaces and scores real evidence of demand. Run for every business.

deliverable.artifact: An opportunity brief with ranked candidate niches and the evidence behind each, written to the company brain.
sections: Aggregated signals from at least three channels
Candidate niches clustered by job and audience
Each candidate scored on urgency, frequency, willingness to pay, reachability, and crowding
Ranked niches with a confidence tag per claim

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when each surfaced opportunity is supported by at least three independent signals from at least two channels rather than a single anecdote, candidates are scored on urgency, willingness to pay, reachability, and crowding, and every claim carries a confidence tag with any unavailable source stated.

playbooks.title: Problem Validation Interviews
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before building anything. Confirms the problem is real and urgent, not just plausible. Skip only if a strong review corpus already substitutes.

deliverable.artifact: A problem-validation report with a tagged quote bank and a Go/No-Go/Pivot decision, written to the company brain.
sections: Tagged verbatim quotes and observed workarounds

playbooks.rubric: Passes only with at least 7 of 10 interviews confirming the problem unprompted and 5 or more participants showing active workarounds, every claim backed by tagged verbatim quotes rather than hypothetical intent answers, ending in a scored Go/No-Go/Pivot decision and a refined ICP.

playbooks.title: Competitive Teardown
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for every business to find defensible whitespace. Not a feature checklist; a strategy and moat diagnosis.

playbooks.title: Market Sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM)
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for every business to confirm the opportunity is large enough to justify the build. Kill if SOM is below threshold.

deliverable.artifact: A market sizing model with TAM/SAM/SOM, sources, and a go/no-go recommendation, written to the company brain.
sections: Inputs (ICP, pricing model, geographic scope, competitor revenues)
TAM, SAM, SOM by two independent methods
The TAM boundary (what the market is not)
VC-grade threshold and unit-economics sanity check

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when TAM, SAM, and SOM are computed by at least two independent methods (top-down and bottom-up) that agree within roughly 3x, every qualitative claim is replaced by a number with a source and a calculation, and a go/no-go is issued against VC-grade thresholds.

playbooks.title: Jobs-to-be-Done Extraction
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when interview data or a review corpus exists. Feeds positioning, roadmap, and the competitive teardown. Needs source material to be useful.

playbooks.title: Red-Team the Thesis
playbooks.selection_hint: The L0 gate. Run before committing capital. Produces the validated thesis and kill criteria that unlock incorporation.

deliverable.artifact: A red-teamed thesis document with a confidence-rated assumption stack and pre-committed numeric kill criteria, written to the company brain.
sections: Assumption stack tagged load-bearing or convenient
Disconfirming evidence and confidence per load-bearing assumption

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when every load-bearing assumption carries disconfirming evidence and a confidence rating, the kill criteria are numeric and pre-committed rather than vague, and the thesis survives its own pre-mortem to earn a defensible Go call.

playbooks.title: Why-Now Memo
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when the thesis is articulated. Produces the timing narrative reused later in the fundraise deck. Skip if only validating internally.

playbooks.title: Beachhead Selection
playbooks.selection_hint: The first L1 commit. Choose one wedge so all force concentrates on a single point. Unlocks positioning and naming.

playbooks.title: Company Naming
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after the wedge is chosen so the name targets the right audience. The name is the most durable IP; do not rush it.

playbooks.title: Domain Acquisition
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the name is final. Secures the primary digital identity. Money and broker negotiation make this a human-gated spend.

playbooks.title: Trademark Clearance
playbooks.selection_hint: Run the knockout search the moment the name is chosen; file once goods/services are defined. Filing is a legal, money, hard-to-reverse action.

playbooks.title: Entity Formation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the venture is committed and you need to bank, contract, hire, or raise. Default Delaware C-Corp; deviations need a documented reason.

deliverable.artifact: An entity-formation record capturing the chosen structure, the filing, the EIN, and the bank account, written to the company brain.
sections: Selected structure with rationale for any deviation from Delaware C-Corp

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the entity structure is chosen against the documented inputs (defaulting to Delaware C-Corp, any deviation justified against a specific condition), the EIN and bank account are recorded, and the binding incorporation step is routed to the founder with any IP, licensing, or jurisdiction risk escalated.

playbooks.title: Founding Docs
playbooks.selection_hint: Run the moment the entity is registered; the 83(b) clock is 30 days. Errors here are fatal to future fundraising.

playbooks.title: ToS & Privacy Policy
playbooks.selection_hint: Required before any product that collects user data goes public. Gates on a formed entity and known data practices. Legal and irreversible; always human-gated.

playbooks.title: Brand Positioning Statement
playbooks.selection_hint: Strategic positioning bet that every content, brand, and trademark step downstream consumes. Run once the MVP slice is scoped.

deliverable.artifact: A brand positioning statement with its five-component canvas and a messaging hierarchy, written to the company brain.
sections: Five components (target customer, need, category, key benefit, differentiation)
Positioning statement in one to three sentences

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when positioning is anchored to the customer and the competitive alternative rather than the feature list, the statement is one to three sentences that deliberately excludes some audiences, and the copy carries no em-dashes or emojis.

playbooks.title: Visual Identity Brief
playbooks.selection_hint: At L3 ship only the Minimum Viable Brand (Inter, one primary color, text wordmark). The full identity here is a post-PMF mature variant; do not run the full build pre-PMF.

playbooks.title: Tone of Voice Guide
playbooks.selection_hint: The voice contract every content-producing agent reads. Run after positioning so all copy speaks consistently.

playbooks.title: MVP Scoping
playbooks.selection_hint: Defines the smallest buildable product after the thesis survives red-teaming. Gates tech stack, positioning, and PRD.

deliverable.artifact: An MVP scope defining the thin slice, the non-goals, and the activation event, written to the company brain.
sections: Single core value the MVP delivers to the beachhead user
Minimum capabilities to deliver it end to end
Activation event and how it is measured

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the slice is the smallest end-to-end path to the core value (buildable in a few weeks, else cut again), the non-goals are named explicitly so scope cannot creep back, and the activation event is defined with how it is measured, holding off full brand or visual identity.

playbooks.title: Tech Stack Selection
playbooks.selection_hint: The infra gate. Pick the stack before hosting, observability, or any build can start. Skip only for businesses that ship no software.

deliverable.artifact: A tech stack specification with per-layer choices and rationale, written to the company brain.
sections: Foundational constraints (boring technology, monolith first, TCO)
Per-layer choice with rationale and rejected alternatives

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when each layer is chosen by explicit rule with its rejected alternatives recorded, any non-standard choice is justified by a specific measurable problem a boring alternative cannot solve, and a competent open-market hire could be productive on the stack within two weeks.

playbooks.title: PRD Drafting
playbooks.selection_hint: The source of truth engineering and design agents execute against. Run per initiative once the MVP slice is scoped and validated.

deliverable.artifact: A validated, testable product requirements document, written to the company brain.
sections: Verified prerequisite inputs with any assumption flagged
Problem, goals, target user, and success metric in narrative

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the PRD is built from validated inputs rather than a feature request or opinion, defines the why and the what without specifying the how, and gives acceptance criteria specific enough that an engineering agent needs no clarifying questions.

playbooks.title: Feature Prioritization
playbooks.selection_hint: The standing decision of what to build next. Runs continuously through build and beyond, not once. Full scoring every two weeks; triage daily.

playbooks.title: Onboarding Flow Design
playbooks.selection_hint: The highest-leverage growth surface. Design it once the product core value and signup flow exist; it feeds the beta program.

deliverable.artifact: A deployed onboarding flow with recovery messaging and an instrumented activation event, written to the company brain.
sections: Defined aha moment and core action event
Flow modeled as a state machine with triggers and recovery nudges
Friction stripped to hold each step drop-off at or below 30 percent

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the flow is oriented around the user's desired outcome rather than the feature list, each single-step drop-off is held at or below 30 percent, and the activation event is instrumented against targets of activation at or above 30 percent within 7 days and week-1 retention at or above 40 percent.

playbooks.title: Usability Testing Protocol
playbooks.selection_hint: Behavioral testing of whether users can accomplish goals. Run on a stable prototype or staging build, repeatedly through build. Not for attitudinal questions.

playbooks.title: Beta Program Management
playbooks.selection_hint: PMF-validation and relationship-building, not QA. Run once the core workflow is functional and instrumented, ahead of public launch.

playbooks.title: Release & Changelog Cadence
playbooks.selection_hint: Decouples deploy from release and turns the changelog into a marketing, retention, and recruiting asset. Stands up once CI/CD exists; then operates continuously.

playbooks.title: Hosting & Deployment Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Stands up the deploy foundation once the stack is chosen. Everything observability, security, backup, and release depends on this landing.

deliverable.artifact: A hosting and deployment record with the live URL, the CI/CD pipeline, and secrets management, written to the company brain.
sections: Hosting choice justified against the decision tree
Provisioned compute and managed database with automated backups
Secrets manager with no hardcoded secrets

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the hosting choice is justified against traffic, budget, and cost-predictability as a first-class criterion, the CI/CD pipeline runs lint, test, build, and deploy with no secret hardcoded in the repo or pipeline, and any ongoing-spend provisioning is escalated to the founder.

playbooks.title: Observability Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a deployed app exists. It is the sensory layer that arms incident response and tells you why something broke, not just that it broke.

playbooks.title: Incident Response
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once observability is live, then it runs always-on. Triggered by any alert, outage, or security event. Never a one-time checkbox.

playbooks.title: Security Baseline
playbooks.selection_hint: Stand up day one alongside hosting. Prevents the cheap, predictable breaches (stolen credentials dominate) and avoids expensive SOC 2 retrofits.

deliverable.artifact: A security baseline documenting auth, secrets, abuse prevention, and agent-tool controls, written to the company brain.
sections: Auth architecture chosen by decision tree
Secrets classified with storage, rotation, and access scope
Rate limiting and abuse prevention on public endpoints
Input validation and output encoding at boundaries
Agent tool scopes and prompt-injection guards

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when secret hygiene is treated as the top control with no human access to production Tier 0/1 secrets and immediate rotation on compromise, public endpoints are rate-limited and validate input, and agent tools are scoped to least privilege.

playbooks.title: Data Backup & Recovery
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once any data store exists. Turns an outage, ransomware event, or bad migration into a 30-minute problem instead of an existential one.

playbooks.title: Analytics Stack Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: The sensory apparatus of the business. Every downstream metric, funnel, cohort, and dashboard depends on it. Set up in week one to avoid an 18-month rebuild.

deliverable.artifact: An analytics stack with instrumented capture, a single warehouse, and a version-controlled metrics layer, written to the company brain.
sections: Single event pipeline to the warehouse
Version-controlled metrics layer with one definition per metric

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when every user action is captured once at the source and routed from a single canonical stream with no tool storing its own copy, every metric has exactly one authoritative definition in version control, and privacy and consent are built in from day one rather than retrofitted.

playbooks.title: Event Taxonomy Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run right after the analytics stack lands. A disciplined taxonomy is what makes funnels, cohorts, and reads trustworthy; without it tracking drifts into chaos.

playbooks.title: North Star Metric Definition
playbooks.selection_hint: Define once early PMF signal exists so every agent optimizes one number. Before PMF, anchor it on retention or core-action completion, not growth.

deliverable.artifact: A north star metric definition with its metric tree, guardrails, and instrumentation plan, written to the company brain.
sections: North star metric statement and the core value exchange
Metric tree of 3-4 MECE input metrics

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the metric is a single value-denominated number that passes all five checklist criteria, decomposes into 3-4 MECE input metrics via a metric tree, and is protected by guardrail counter-metrics; before PMF it must anchor on retention or core-action completion rather than growth.

playbooks.title: Funnel Analysis
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once events flow and there are users moving through the funnel. Diagnoses where the funnel leaks and produces testable remediation hypotheses.

deliverable.artifact: A funnel analysis with the activation metric, the diagnosed drop-offs, and prioritized remediation hypotheses, written to the company brain.
sections: Activation metric and the setup, aha, and habit moments
Funnel by stage segmented by channel, device, geo, and persona
Drop-offs cross-referenced against deploys and campaigns
Top two testable hypotheses with control/variant, MDE, and sample size

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when drop-offs are statistically significant and cross-referenced against the deploy and campaign schedule before being called bottlenecks, bottlenecks are prioritized with RICE, and the top two are handed off as testable hypotheses with a defined MDE and sample size rather than peeked at early.

playbooks.title: Cohort & Retention Analysis
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a critical event and a usage interval are definable. Retention is the most leveraged metric; a 10% lift beats a 10% acquisition lift many times over.

deliverable.artifact: A retention report with cohort tables and the identified critical event, written to the company brain.
sections: D1/D7/D30/W12 cohort tables classified smile or declining
Cohorts segmented by channel and feature use

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the critical event is the value action chosen by retention-delta analysis rather than a generic login proxy, the retention metric type matches the usage pattern, and every curve shift is attributed to a specific intervention or external event via the intervention log.

playbooks.title: Attribution Modeling
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before scaling paid spend, once there is meaningful spend history and conversion volume. Attribution is causal inference, not a reporting exercise.

playbooks.title: CEO Dashboard Build
playbooks.selection_hint: Build once the NSM is defined and data sources are connected. Gives the founder one health screen that alerts on exception rather than being checked.

playbooks.title: GA4 Reading Playbook
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once GA4 (ideally with a BigQuery export) is live and there is traffic to read, typically from launch. Skip products that do not use GA4.

playbooks.title: Mixpanel Reading Playbook
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once Mixpanel has events flowing and a tracking plan exists, typically from launch. Skip products that do not use Mixpanel.

playbooks.title: Weekly Business Review
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once data flows and metrics have owners, then it runs always-on every week. The primary mechanism against operational drift. Never cancelled.

playbooks.title: Pricing Research
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any packaging or pricing-page decision, once positioning and an ICP exist. Skip only for permanently-free products.

deliverable.artifact: A pricing research report with a defensible price range and a recommended value metric, written to the company brain.
sections: Synthesized price range and value metric with confidence tags

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the price range is triangulated across multiple complementary methods rather than a single survey, competitor benchmarking sets the band tested, and every number carries a confidence tag with any unavailable data source stated.

playbooks.title: Packaging & Tier Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after pricing research lands a price range and value metric. The structural decision that pricing-page copy and freemium choice both depend on.

playbooks.title: Pricing Page Copy & Layout
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once positioning and tiers are locked, never before, or the page gets rewritten around placeholder prices. The highest-intent page on the site.

playbooks.title: Freemium / Trial Decision
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after pricing research, before launch. Decides the shape of the acquisition funnel. Most relevant to self-serve software; less so to high-touch services.

playbooks.title: Discount & Promo Policy
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once a list price and gross margin exist, before the sales motion starts giving discounts. A standing guardrail, not a one-off campaign.

playbooks.title: Positioning Canvas
playbooks.selection_hint: The strategic root of all GTM. Run once a beachhead is committed; every pricing, launch, and content decision derives from it.

playbooks.title: Category Design Decision
playbooks.selection_hint: Run after positioning when the market frame is genuinely in question. Skip if the category is obvious and the play is clearly to win share in it.

playbooks.title: Launch Plan (T-90)
playbooks.selection_hint: The conductor of the launch level. Run once product, positioning, and pricing are ready; it sequences PH, HN, PR, and podcast plays under one countdown.

deliverable.artifact: A T-90 launch plan with a dated countdown, RACI, asset inventory, war-room runbook, and metrics dashboard, written to the company brain.
sections: RACI of agent and human owners

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the launch is tier-classified to set lead time, every step has a date, an owner, and an asset, the burst sequences Product Hunt then Hacker News then PR across separate days, and the public launch is founder-gated before T-0.

playbooks.title: Product Hunt Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run under the launch plan when the product is live with no waitlist and the audience is the tech community. Skip for local services and pure offline plays.

playbooks.title: Hacker News Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run under the launch plan only when the product appeals to a technical audience and the submitting account is aged. Skip for non-technical B2C.

playbooks.title: PR & Press Launch
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for any public announcement with a real narrative. Owned media leads; earned media amplifies. Hold wire distribution until Series A.

playbooks.title: Podcast Tour
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when a speaker has 4+ hours a week for 8 weeks and conversion infrastructure exists. A high-trust, zero-media-spend channel; not a branded podcast.

playbooks.title: Community Seeding
playbooks.selection_hint: Run from L3 when owned audience is under ~500 and a landing page or demo exists. Value-first participation; never a paid-ads or owned-community play.

playbooks.title: SEO Keyword Research
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any content or SEO work. Foundation that every content and programmatic-SEO playbook consumes. Skip only if SEO is not a planned channel.

playbooks.title: Content Calendar
playbooks.selection_hint: The operating engine for content. Install once keywords and personas exist; then run continuously to plan, brief, publish, and refresh.

playbooks.title: Pillar + Cluster Content
playbooks.selection_hint: Use to build topical authority on a target domain. Run after keywords exist; produces the linkable long-form assets later channels and link-building depend on.

playbooks.title: Long-form Blog Post
playbooks.selection_hint: The primary organic acquisition unit. Run per keyword cluster once research exists; produces the linkable assets link-building later needs.

playbooks.title: Programmatic SEO
playbooks.selection_hint: Only when a keyword pattern has aggregate volume and a unique dataset can populate 100+ genuinely distinct pages. Scale only post-PMF; thin pages at scale risk sitewide deindexation.

playbooks.title: Technical SEO Audit
playbooks.selection_hint: Run quarterly, after migrations, after core updates, or on a traffic drop. Keeps the site crawlable and indexable so content and programmatic SEO can rank.

playbooks.title: Link Building
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once long-form linkable assets exist and a domain is warmed. Safe earlier, but compounds best post-PMF. Never outreach without a linkable asset to point at.

playbooks.title: Newsletter Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: The owned, algorithm-independent audience channel. Stand up once an ESP and authenticated domain exist; then run continuously. Other channels feed it.

playbooks.title: YouTube Strategy
playbooks.selection_hint: A compounding video channel where packaging and retention drive distribution. Run continuously if video fits the audience and the team can sustain production.

playbooks.title: TikTok Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: B2C / consumer channel. Short-form video where the first 3 seconds decide reach. Skip for pure B2B; run continuously when the audience is on TikTok.

playbooks.title: Twitter / X Content Engine
playbooks.selection_hint: Real-time audience and reply-surface channel; strong for founder-led and tech audiences. Run continuously where the ICP is active on X.

playbooks.title: LinkedIn Organic
playbooks.selection_hint: B2B channel. Founder-led authority content into a high-intent professional audience; feeds the dark funnel. Skip for pure B2C. Gates LinkedIn Ads (070) later.

playbooks.title: Instagram Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: B2C / consumer channel. Reels-first acquisition with DM funnels replacing link-in-bio. Skip for pure B2B; run continuously when the audience is on Instagram.

playbooks.title: Reddit Marketing
playbooks.selection_hint: Community-first channel with evergreen SEO and heavy AI-citation weight. Run continuously where the ICP has active subreddits. Requires a warmed account; never spam.

playbooks.title: Google Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once analytics, event taxonomy, and attribution exist. Demand capture for categories where buyers already search; needs conversion data to optimize.

playbooks.title: Meta Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once analytics, event taxonomy, and attribution exist, and Business Manager plus pixel/CAPI (EMQ high) are live. Strong for B2C/DTC volume.

playbooks.title: LinkedIn Ads
playbooks.selection_hint: Only with ACV >= $5k, budget >= $3k/mo, audience >= 50k, and CAPI. CPM economics are fatal below the ACV floor; a B2C or low-ACV company never runs this.

playbooks.title: Retargeting
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once a live page has audience volume (>= 1000 visitors/30d) and the landing page is optimized. Harvests existing demand; never a brand-building channel.

playbooks.title: Creative Testing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run continuously alongside active paid social once CPA targets are set. Creative drives ~56% of paid ROI; volume and speed of iteration are the structural advantage.

playbooks.title: Landing Page CRO
playbooks.selection_hint: Run on a live page with traffic or qualitative signal. The pre-flight gate for paid: scaling spend onto an unoptimized page wastes budget.

playbooks.title: Affiliate Program Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Gated on M1 retention >= 40%, 50+ paying customers, and a clean funnel. An affiliate program is a multiplier for existing momentum, never a primary early channel.

playbooks.title: Influencer Partnerships
playbooks.selection_hint: Run post-PMF once unit economics are validated and a conversion path exists. Strongest for B2C/DTC; the mechanism is trust transfer, so audience fit and authenticity beat reach.

playbooks.title: Integration & Co-marketing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once you have 50+ closed-won accounts, engineering bandwidth, and CRM partner fields. Ecosystem-led growth compounds where paid cannot; B2B SaaS oriented.

playbooks.title: Referral Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Gated on PMF. Never run before retention proves out; a referral loop on a leaky bucket amplifies churn and burns treasury.

playbooks.title: Welcome Email Sequence
playbooks.selection_hint: Highest-leverage lifecycle asset. Run once deliverability is warmed and the aha event is instrumented; it escorts new users to activation.

playbooks.title: Nurture Campaign
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a CRM, lead scoring, and a content library exist. Behavior-responsive education for prospects who have raised their hand but are not yet ready to buy.

playbooks.title: Win-back Campaign
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once you have customers with churn or dormancy events and warmed deliverability. Recovers former customers at 5 to 25x lower cost than new acquisition.

playbooks.title: Transactional Emails
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the product has user accounts and deliverability is warmed. These are the highest-open emails you own; treat as product surface, not marketing.

playbooks.title: Email Deliverability Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Critical-path L2 item. Start day one because the warmup window is 30 to 45 days; every email playbook (078/079/080/081/084) is blocked until this is warmed.

playbooks.title: ICP & Target Account Listing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before any outbound for a sales-led or hybrid B2B motion. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve products with no named-account targeting. Refresh on ICP drift (CAC up, churn up, win rate down).

playbooks.title: Outbound Email Sequences
playbooks.selection_hint: The outbound engine for a B2B sales-led motion. Needs warmed sending domains (082) and a target account list (083). Skip for pure B2C or self-serve.

playbooks.title: Discovery Call Framework
playbooks.selection_hint: The first substantive sales conversation in a B2B sales-led motion. Fires when a prospect accepts a meeting. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve with no human sales call.

playbooks.title: Demo Script
playbooks.selection_hint: The persuasion event after discovery qualifies a B2B deal. Fires only when discovery is complete and a sandbox is ready. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve with no live demo.

playbooks.title: Objection Handling Library
playbooks.selection_hint: A callable, always-on library invoked by discovery (085), demo (086), and close (088) whenever a prospect resists. Install once a B2B sales motion exists. Not run standalone.

playbooks.title: Contract & Close
playbooks.selection_hint: The commercial/legal close for a B2B deal, triggered by a verbal yes. Skip for pure B2C or self-serve checkout. The binding contract is a human gate.

deliverable.artifact: A deal-close record with a mutual action plan and the signed contract terms, written to the company brain.
sections: Economic buyer and decision/paper process mapped
Mutual action plan with milestones and owners
Procurement, security, and legal redline status

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the economic buyer and paper process are mapped, procurement, security, and legal run in parallel rather than single-threaded, every concession is traded for a return rather than conceded, and the binding agreement is explicitly approved by the founder before signature.

playbooks.title: Support Workflow
playbooks.selection_hint: Install the moment the first paying customer exists. Applies to any business with customers, B2B or B2C. Operate continuously, not a one-time checkbox.

playbooks.title: Churn Diagnosis & Win-back
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once there is recurring revenue and billing webhooks. Highest-ROI lever is recovering involuntary (failed-payment) churn. Operate continuously.

deliverable.artifact: A churn diagnosis with per-account root cause and tailored win-back campaigns, written to the company brain.
sections: Churn classified on the avoidable/unavoidable by expected/unexpected matrix
Involuntary recovery via dunning, card-updater, and retry
Voluntary churn branched by lifecycle timing
Win-back tailored to reason and timing

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when every churn event is traced to a root cause before any intervention, involuntary failed-payment churn is recovered as the highest-ROI lever, and win-backs are tailored to the churn reason and timing rather than reusing one template across all churn.

playbooks.title: NPS / CSAT Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once there are active customers and a survey channel. Applies to any business, B2B or B2C. Operate on a fixed cadence; promoters feed the case-study pipeline.

playbooks.title: Case Study & Testimonial Pipeline
playbooks.selection_hint: Install once customers have measurable outcomes and an NPS signal exists. Applies to any business, B2B or B2C. Publishing a customer's story requires their consent.

playbooks.title: A/B Testing Protocol
playbooks.selection_hint: Activate once a surface has live traffic (about 100+ users/day). Below that, use qualitative learning instead, not A/B tests.

playbooks.title: Growth Loop Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Run first in scale acquisition, before scaling paid spend. Needs market-product fit and channel data already in hand.

playbooks.title: Pricing and Packaging Experimentation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when there are live paying customers (about 100+ willingness-to-pay respondents reachable). Highest-leverage profit lever, but every change passes a founder gate.

playbooks.title: Financial Model and Forecast
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when 12 to 24 months of actuals exist and a raise or board cadence is near. Consumes unit economics; produces the model the fundraise leans on.

playbooks.title: Unit Economics
playbooks.selection_hint: Baseline at L5 once customer-level billing and channel spend exist. The single answer to "do we make money on one customer?" Feeds the model and the raise.

deliverable.artifact: A unit-economics model with cohort CAC, gross margin, realized LTV, contribution margin, and payback by channel, written to the company brain.
sections: Realized cohort LTV over a capped horizon
Payback and LTV-to-CAC by cohort and channel

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when CAC is paid-only, fully loaded, and time-lagged (never blended) for scaling decisions, LTV is realized over a capped horizon rather than an infinite-horizon projection, and every number is segmented by cohort and channel rather than blended into an average that hides failing channels.

playbooks.title: Fundraise Deck and Investor Outreach
playbooks.selection_hint: Only when raising. Needs PMF or a real milestone, a defensible model, and case studies. Skip entirely if staying unfunded.

playbooks.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Gates
playbooks.selection_hint: Installed day zero before Level 0, before any agent takes any action. The safety wrapper around every other playbook. Never skip.

playbooks.title: Marketplace Trust and Safety
playbooks.selection_hint: Run before opening a marketplace to real transactions. Liquidity without trust is churn; the first bad transaction without a dispute path is a dead marketplace.

deliverable.artifact: A trust and safety policy covering identity, fraud screening, disputes, and enforcement for both marketplace sides, written to the company brain.
sections: Identity and onboarding requirements per side
Fraud screening signals, thresholds, and review queue
Escrow and payout terms routed to Capx Pay

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when a dispute path exists before any transaction opens, identity and screening are proportional to transaction risk on each side, the enforcement ladder is explicit, and all funds movement is routed to Capx Pay rather than handled in the policy.

playbooks.title: Marketplace Liquidity Balancing
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the marketplace has live transactions and a measurable match rate. The single most important marketplace growth lever; balance the short side before spending on the long side.

deliverable.artifact: A liquidity plan stating current match rate and time-to-fill, the short side, and the levers in flight, updated each cycle.
sections: Match rate, time-to-fill, and fill rate by segment
Identified short side and shortest segments

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the short side is identified from measured match rate and time-to-fill, the plan concentrates on one segment to saturation rather than spreading thin, and it never adds demand to a supply-starved market in a way that raises churn.

playbooks.title: Local Google Business Profile and Maps
playbooks.selection_hint: Run early for any local service. For a local business this is the primary discovery channel, ahead of a website or paid ads.

deliverable.artifact: A local presence record covering the verified Google Business Profile, the booking path, and NAP-consistent directories, written to the company brain.
sections: Categories, service area, hours, and photos

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the profile is verified with every field completed, the same name, address, and phone are mirrored identically across directories, a map view has a working path to a booking or call, and a review-request flow fires on job completion.

playbooks.title: Local Reviews and Reputation
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a local service has repeat customers. Reviews are the compounding local-ranking and trust lever; start the loop as early as you have happy customers.

playbooks.title: B2B Security and SOC2 Readiness
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when enterprise deals start stalling on security review, or before moving upmarket. Security review is a gate on B2B revenue once deal sizes grow.

playbooks.title: App Store Optimization and Mobile Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for a consumer mobile app around launch. The store listing is the top of the mobile funnel; optimize it before spending on installs.

playbooks.title: Pre-seed Fundraise Narrative
playbooks.selection_hint: Run early for a founder who intends to raise, before revenue or PMF exist. The pre-seed raise sells the team and the insight, not metrics; do not wait for traction the round does not require.

playbooks.title: Supplier Sourcing & COGS
playbooks.selection_hint: The first physical-goods gate. No price, packaging, or unit-economics number is trustworthy until landed COGS per SKU is modeled from real supplier quotes.

playbooks.title: Inventory & Fulfillment Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once suppliers and landed COGS exist. Operational backbone for delivering physical product; reorder points and SLAs prevent both stockouts and dead capital.

playbooks.title: Shipping & Returns Policy
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once fulfillment can quote real shipping costs and lead times. The customer-facing promise on delivery and returns; it sets expectations and protects margin.

playbooks.title: Repeat Purchase & AOV
playbooks.selection_hint: The physical-goods retention loop. Run once orders and events are instrumented; repeat rate and AOV expansion decide whether paid acquisition is ever profitable.

playbooks.title: Merchandising & Catalog
playbooks.selection_hint: Situational. Reach for it once enough SKUs exist that structure, bundling, and collection strategy change discovery and AOV; skip for a single-product catalog.

playbooks.title: Activation Rate Optimization
playbooks.selection_hint: The first compounding gate after launch. A launched product lives or dies on whether new users reach value, so this is the metric to watch once signups exist, not top-of-funnel acquisition.

playbooks.title: First-Run Aha Experience
playbooks.selection_hint: Define the destination before building or measuring the path. Run once the PRD and core value are set, so onboarding design, activation optimization, and retention all measure against one aha.

playbooks.title: Hardware Certification & Compliance
playbooks.selection_hint: A hard legal gate for selling physical product. An uncertified device cannot lawfully ship in its target markets, and shipping one risks seizure, recall, and liability. Start the testing early because lab queues and retests are slow.

deliverable.artifact: A hardware compliance file with passed test reports, certificates, marks, and a Declaration of Conformity proving the device is legal to sell, written to the company brain.
sections: Compliance plan with standards, labs, lead times, and costs
Technical file and Declaration of Conformity

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when certification is against the production-representative unit that will actually ship, every required regime per target market has a passed report, no compliance claim or mark is applied before its report passes, and the founder has approved the markets, lab spend, and published claims.

playbooks.title: KYC/AML Program
playbooks.selection_hint: The do-or-die gate for any crypto business that custodies or transmits customer value. No onboarding, deposit, or transfer can go live until identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction monitoring are operating.

deliverable.artifact: A documented KYC/AML compliance program covering identity, screening, monitoring, the travel rule, and named ownership, written to the company brain.
sections: Regulatory perimeter and obligations per activity
KYC/CIP identity and verification policy by tier
Named compliance owner and governance cadence

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when no customer value can move before identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction monitoring are live, screening is continuous rather than one-time, and a named human owns and signs off on the program.

playbooks.title: Smart Contract Audit
playbooks.selection_hint: The do-or-die gate before mainnet. A contract that holds user funds is unforgiving and immutable once deployed; an unaudited deploy is an unbounded loss waiting to happen. Run before any value-bearing contract ships.

deliverable.artifact: A mainnet deploy gate record with the third-party audit, the verified invariants, and a live bug bounty, written to the company brain.
sections: Frozen audit scope and pinned commit
Findings triaged with remediation and re-audit evidence
Live bug bounty with scope and payout tiers
Founder sign-off on the deploy gate

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when no value-bearing contract reaches mainnet with an unresolved critical or high finding, the audited artifact is the exact deployed commit (re-audited after any post-audit change), critical invariants are formally verified rather than only tested, and the bounty complements rather than substitutes for the audit.

playbooks.title: Token & Licensing Strategy
playbooks.selection_hint: Decide the regulatory shape of the business, which licenses to hold or avoid, how the token is classified, and which jurisdictions to serve. Operating unlicensed where a license is required becomes existential at scale.

playbooks.title: Hardware Prototyping & DFM
playbooks.selection_hint: The first hardware build gate. No contract manufacturer, certification, or production quote is real until a working prototype and a costed, manufacturable BOM exist.

playbooks.title: Contract Manufacturing Setup
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a released design package and a costed BOM exist. Turns a validated design into repeatable units at volume. The tooling and CM choice are expensive to reverse, so qualify before you commit.

playbooks.title: Dunning & Involuntary Churn Recovery
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once recurring charges are live. Involuntary churn (failed cards, not cancellations) is commonly 20 to 40 percent of total churn and the cheapest to recover, because the customer never chose to leave.

playbooks.title: Marketplace Supply Acquisition
playbooks.selection_hint: The hard side of a marketplace. Supply (sellers, providers, hosts, creators) is usually the binding constraint; concentrate it in one beachhead so early demand always finds a fillable match.

playbooks.title: Ad Revenue and Yield
playbooks.selection_hint: The money mechanism for an ad-supported product. Once there is engagement to monetize, fill rate and eCPM are the levers between DAU and revenue, so this is the revenue work for an ad model, not a side task.

playbooks.title: Hardware Preorder and Demand Validation
playbooks.selection_hint: The gate before capital-intensive hardware spend. Tooling and a first production run are largely irreversible, so prove paid demand with preorders or crowdfunding before sinking money into supply, not after.

playbooks.title: Hiring and Org Scaling
playbooks.selection_hint: The ready target the moment the founder's do-or-die constraint is hiring capacity. Run when product and demand work but headcount caps output; skip while the team is still finding the product.

playbooks.title: Account Expansion and Upsell
playbooks.selection_hint: The offense side of net revenue retention. Run once there is recurring revenue and a retained base; the cheapest revenue is expansion inside accounts the company already serves. Distinct from churn defense.

playbooks.title: Services Delivery and Utilization
playbooks.selection_hint: The operating core of a services firm. Run once the business delivers paid engagements; billable utilization is the metric the whole business turns on, so this is monitored, not a one-time setup.

playbooks.title: On-chain Payment & Stablecoin Integration
playbooks.selection_hint: The payment rail itself for a crypto product. No deposit, settlement, or payout can flow until wallet-connect, a stablecoin rail, gas handling, and an on/off-ramp are live. Build before any value moves.

deliverable.artifact: An on-chain payment rail spec covering chain, settlement asset, wallet model, fees, ramps, and reconciliation, written to the company brain.
sections: Settlement stablecoin and pinned token addresses
On/off-ramp with region and KYC coverage

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the rail is non-custodial by default, no unconfirmed transaction is ever treated as settled, token addresses and ramp keys are configuration with named owners rather than literals in source, and ramp access is gated on the KYC/AML screening.

playbooks.title: Wallet Onboarding UX
playbooks.selection_hint: The make-or-break funnel for any self-serve crypto product. Wallet creation, key recovery, and the first signed transaction are where most users drop; design this once the payment rail exists.

playbooks.title: Tokenomics Design
playbooks.selection_hint: Only for a business that actually issues its own token. Sets utility, supply, emissions, and vesting before allocations are locked. Skip entirely if there is no token; the design is irreversible once distribution begins.

playbooks.title: On-chain Community & Incentive Growth
playbooks.selection_hint: The crypto-native acquisition loop once a product and wallet onboarding exist. Points, airdrops, quests, and on-chain referral, with sybil resistance so the budget reaches real users. Run as a recurring campaign cadence.

playbooks.title: On-chain Analytics & Attribution
playbooks.selection_hint: The measurement layer for a crypto product, where the source of truth is the chain, not a server log. Wallet cohorts, on-chain funnels, retention by wallet, and source attribution. Run once an analytics stack and on-chain activity exist.

playbooks.title: Protocol Security Monitoring
playbooks.selection_hint: The live-defense counterpart to the one-time audit. Continuous monitoring, exploit detection, a guardian pause, and a rehearsed hack runbook for any value-bearing contract that is live. Run from mainnet onward, as a standing capability.

deliverable.artifact: A live protocol monitoring and incident-response setup with invariant monitors, a guardian pause, and a rehearsed hack runbook, written to the company brain.
sections: Alerting thresholds tuned against normal activity
Guardian pause capability with quorum and conditions
Hack incident runbook with named on-call owners

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when the pause capability is rehearsed before it is needed, monitors track the deployed artifact and are re-baselined after every change, and the hack response is decided in advance with a named on-call owner rather than improvised during a live drain.

playbooks.title: Treasury & Stablecoin Runway
playbooks.selection_hint: The survival discipline for a crypto business holding its own funds on-chain. Hold runway in stablecoins, secure it behind a multisig, and diversify custody and issuer exposure. Run once the treasury holds meaningful value.

deliverable.artifact: A treasury policy covering balance buckets, stablecoin runway, multisig custody, and runway alerts, written to the company brain.
sections: Stablecoin runway policy with issuer and chain diversification limits
Multisig custody setup with quorum and signer procedures

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when runway is measured in stablecoins rather than a volatile native token, no single key or single person can move treasury funds, issuer, chain, and custody are diversified so one failure cannot end the company, and custody or concentration changes are founder-gated.

playbooks.title: Open Source Strategy
playbooks.selection_hint: The defining strategic choice for a developer tool with a bottom-up motion. License and open-core boundary are nearly irreversible once a public repo and a contributor base exist, so settle them before the first public commit.

playbooks.title: API and SDK Design
playbooks.selection_hint: The product surface for any developer tool. Run once the open/commercial boundary and stack are set, because the public API contract is the hardest thing in the company to change after adoption.

playbooks.title: Developer Docs and DX
playbooks.selection_hint: For a developer tool, docs are the product surface where adoption is won or lost. Run once the API contract exists; treat docs as a living funnel, not a one-time deliverable.

playbooks.title: Developer Relations and DevRel
playbooks.selection_hint: The primary acquisition engine for a developer tool, where engineers trust other engineers over ads. Run once docs and a real quickstart exist; measure devrel by activated developers, not vanity reach.

playbooks.title: Developer Community and Contributors
playbooks.selection_hint: For an OSS or developer tool, the community is both the support layer and the contribution engine. Run once the open-source posture is set; governance and a contributor path are what turn users into maintainers.

playbooks.title: Bottom-Up PLG to Sales Handoff
playbooks.selection_hint: The revenue bridge for a bottom-up developer tool. Run once self-serve activation and a community exist; the point is catching the moment a self-serve account becomes a sales-worthy deal without smothering the self-serve motion.

playbooks.title: Firmware & OTA Updates
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for any connected device that ships firmware. A device with no secure update path cannot be patched for a security flaw or a field bug, and a bad push with no rollback bricks the installed base. Build the update channel before the device ships, not after the first recall.

playbooks.title: Hardware QA & Reliability
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a production line exists. Reliability is what turns a unit that works on the bench into one that survives years in the field. Unmeasured defect rates surface as a flood of returns and a destroyed reputation after launch, when fixing them is most expensive.

playbooks.title: Retail & Channel Distribution
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the device is certified and producible at volume and direct demand is proven. Retail and distribution multiply reach but take a margin and impose terms; enter deliberately with priced channel economics and a MAP policy, not by accepting the first distributor that calls.

playbooks.title: Warranty & RMA Operations
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once units are in customers' hands. A hardware company without an RMA pipeline turns every defect into a refund and a lost customer. Warranty is both a customer-trust promise and a financial liability that must be reserved for and managed.

playbooks.title: Spare Parts & Aftermarket
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a model has an installed base and an RMA pipeline. The aftermarket (spares, consumables, accessories, refurbished units) is a high-margin revenue stream and a service obligation; it also governs how a model is retired without stranding the customers who own it.

playbooks.title: PLG Instrumentation and PQL Scoring
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once events flow and an activation moment is definable. In a self-serve motion the product is the top of the funnel, so the PQL score is the routing signal for in-product prompts, lifecycle, and any human follow-up. Skip if the motion is purely sales-led with no free or trial usage.

playbooks.title: In-App Purchase and Store Economics
playbooks.selection_hint: Run for any consumer app that monetizes through the mobile stores. The store fee and the subscription-vs-consumable mix decide whether the unit economics close, so this gates how the paywall is priced and packaged. Skip if monetization is web-only outside the stores.

playbooks.title: Paywall and Pricing Experiments
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a paywall is live and conversion volume supports a test. The paywall is the single highest-leverage revenue surface in a self-serve app, and small placement, gating, and trial changes move trial-to-paid more than most acquisition work. Skip if monetization is sales-led with no in-product purchase moment.

playbooks.title: Referral and Virality Loops
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once activation is solid and users reach value, because a referral loop amplifies a working product and a broken one alike. A self-serve product with a sharable output or a multiplayer reason to invite gets the most leverage. Skip until activation is fixed.

playbooks.title: Mobile Lifecycle and Push Re-engagement
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the app is live and retention is being measured. For a consumer app the D1/D7/D30 curve is the business, and lifecycle messaging is the main lever that bends it short of changing the product. Skip if there is no retained-use loop to reinforce.

playbooks.title: First Users by Hand
playbooks.selection_hint: The first-users move for a consumer product with no users. Do things that do not scale, hand-recruiting the first cohort from the communities they already live in, before spending a dollar on paid.

deliverable.artifact: A first-users plan and log, naming the communities, the hand-to-hand tactic, and the first cohort recruited, written to the company brain.
sections: The 3 to 5 communities or channels where the audience already gathers
The hand-to-hand tactic per channel (post, DM, waitlist, in-person)
The first cohort recruited (count and how)
What they did in the product and where they dropped

playbooks.rubric: Passes only when a real first cohort has been recruited by hand from named communities and their early behaviour is recorded, not when a generic "post on social and run ads" plan is written.

playbooks.title: Willingness-to-Pay Research
playbooks.selection_hint: Choose before setting any price. Strongest when you have target accounts identified but no revenue and no defended price point yet.

deliverable.artifact: wtp_evidence
sections: Price band by segment with quotes

playbooks.rubric: Passes if the value metric is named, a defensible price band per segment is supported by at least eight buyer quotes, and the budget owner is identified.

playbooks.title: Revenue Model Selection
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once the value proposition is clear and before pricing or financial modeling; surface it early so the charging mechanism is chosen, not inherited.

deliverable.artifact: revenue_model_choice
sections: Why this fits the value delivered

playbooks.rubric: Passes if it names one primary model with a specific charging metric, ties it to how value is delivered and consumed, and shows a per-unit margin sketch that does not lose money at scale.

playbooks.title: Pricing Smoke Test
playbooks.selection_hint: Select before launch when price is a guess and you have access to prospects or traffic. Skip if pricing is fixed by a marketplace take rate or a contract you do not control.
playbooks.typical_milestone: pre-launch pricing validated with a real money or intent signal

deliverable.artifact: pricing_signal

playbooks.rubric: Strong if the recommended price is backed by a real money or click-through signal from at least 30 target prospects, not opinion or guesswork.

playbooks.title: Pre-Sales and Letters of Intent
playbooks.selection_hint: Select for B2B builders pre-revenue who have a validated problem and a target account list but no signed demand yet. Skip for self-serve consumer or pure transactional plays where individual contracts are not how buyers commit.

deliverable.artifact: loi_pipeline_tracker

playbooks.rubric: Passes if at least three named target accounts signed an LOI or paid-pilot agreement with a dated commitment and a recorded price, tracked in the pipeline.

playbooks.title: Design Partner Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when you have an ICP and validated problem but no paying customers, and the product is early enough that real users must shape it. Best for high-ACV b2b where a handful of committed accounts matters more than volume.
playbooks.typical_milestone: First paid commitments from named accounts

deliverable.artifact: design_partner_agreement

playbooks.rubric: Passes if 3 or more named accounts have signed an agreement with a real dollar commitment and a defined feedback cadence, not a free pilot.

playbooks.title: Naming and Brand Architecture
playbooks.selection_hint: Run when the company or product is unnamed, when a placeholder name is leaking into copy, or when a second product line forces a parent-versus-standalone branding choice.

deliverable.artifact: brand_name_decision
sections: Architecture decision (branded house vs house of brands)

playbooks.rubric: Passes if one name is locked with documented trademark and domain clearance, and the architecture rule states how the next product gets named.

playbooks.title: Messaging Pillars and Proof
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once positioning is locked and before writing website, deck, or ad copy. Skip if positioning is still unsettled or no customer evidence exists yet.

deliverable.artifact: messaging_house

playbooks.rubric: Passes if each of 3 to 4 pillars ties directly to the positioning and carries at least two specific, verifiable proof points rather than adjectives.

playbooks.title: Category Design and Point of View
playbooks.selection_hint: Run this when you have positioning and a validated problem but the market still sorts you into an existing crowded category. Skip if you are a clear fast-follower competing on price or execution inside a defined category.

deliverable.artifact: category_definition
sections: Old game and its broken metric
Proof spine: three claims and their assets

playbooks.rubric: Passes if a stranger can read the pov_thesis and correctly state the new category, the named enemy, and the single metric buyers should judge it on.

playbooks.title: Brand Guidelines System
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once a positioning statement and a working logo exist and output is starting to fan out across surfaces (site, deck, social, product UI).

deliverable.artifact: brand_system

playbooks.rubric: Passes if a contributor who has never seen the brand can produce an on-brand surface using only the document, with named tokens, a fixed type scale, explicit logo rules, and concrete voice examples.

playbooks.title: Founder Brand and Thought Leadership
playbooks.selection_hint: Select when the founder has earned a point of view in the category and distribution is the bottleneck, not the product. Skip if the founder cannot commit to a weekly cadence for at least eight weeks.

deliverable.artifact: founder_content_system

playbooks.rubric: Passes if there is a named platform, three to five defensible pillars, a committed weekly cadence with a drafted backlog, and a working subscribe path that captured at least one real subscriber.

playbooks.title: Customer Onboarding and Success Activation
playbooks.selection_hint: Select when deals close with a human touch and customers need help reaching value, not when activation is fully self-serve inside the product.

deliverable.artifact: success_onboarding

playbooks.rubric: Pass if a named owner can run a kickoff, produce a per-customer success plan, and report each account's time-to-first-value against a defined milestone within the first two weeks.

playbooks.title: Lifecycle and Retention Email Program
playbooks.selection_hint: Select once you have live users and a defined activation event but retention is leaking after signup. Skip if you have not yet shipped a product people can return to.

deliverable.artifact: lifecycle_retention_program

playbooks.rubric: Passes if every message is tied to a named trigger event and segment, and an activation rate target with a holdout control group is defined.

playbooks.title: Renewal and Retention Playbook
playbooks.selection_hint: Run once recurring revenue exists and the first cohort of contracts is approaching renewal. Skip for one-time transactional models.
playbooks.typical_milestone: First renewal cohort approaching contract end

deliverable.artifact: renewal_motion

playbooks.rubric: Passes if every active account carries a current health score and a dated renewal owner, and at least three triggered save plays are documented with entry criteria.

playbooks.title: Customer Advisory Board
playbooks.selection_hint: Pick this once you have 15 or more paying accounts and a roadmap with real tradeoffs. Skip if you are pre-revenue or selling self-serve to long-tail consumers where a small board cannot represent the base.
playbooks.typical_milestone: post-pmf scaling, expanding from 15 to 100+ accounts

deliverable.artifact: advisory_board

playbooks.rubric: Strong if the board has a written mandate, 8 to 12 ICP-representative members on terms, and a documented loop from session feedback to roadmap decisions to named advocates.

playbooks.title: Lead Qualification Framework and Deal Scoring
playbooks.selection_hint: Select when a founder-led or early sales team is taking discovery calls but lacks a consistent way to decide which deals to pursue and which to drop.
playbooks.typical_milestone: First repeatable sales motion with qualified pipeline

deliverable.artifact: qualification_framework

playbooks.rubric: Passes if a rep can independently score a live deal in under five minutes and reach the same qualify or disqualify decision a peer would.

playbooks.title: Sales Pipeline and CRM Hygiene
playbooks.selection_hint: Select once a B2B team is running multiple live deals and pipeline review is guesswork. Skip for pure self-serve or transactional flows with no human selling.
playbooks.typical_milestone: First repeatable B2B sales motion with more than five concurrent deals

deliverable.artifact: pipeline_definition
sections: Entry and exit criteria per stage

playbooks.rubric: Pass if every stage has a buyer-verifiable exit criterion and a weighted forecast can be produced from the CRM without manual guessing.

playbooks.title: Contractor and IP Assignment Agreements
playbooks.selection_hint: Select once anyone other than a single sole owner has touched code, design, or brand work, and always before opening a fundraise or signing a customer. Skip only if the entity does not yet exist.

deliverable.artifact: ip_assignment_agreements

playbooks.rubric: Pass if every person who created code, design, or brand assets, including founders and pre-incorporation contributors, has a signed agreement assigning that work to the company.

playbooks.title: Data Processing Agreement and Subprocessor Package
playbooks.selection_hint: Select when you process personal data on behalf of business customers and a buyer has asked for a DPA, subprocessor list, or security questionnaire. Skip if you have no business customers handling personal data yet.
playbooks.typical_milestone: First B2B contract requiring a data processing agreement

deliverable.artifact: dpa_package

playbooks.rubric: Passes if a B2B buyer's procurement team can countersign the DPA and clear the subprocessor and data-flow questions without requesting structural redlines.

playbooks.title: Regulatory and Licensing Roadmap
playbooks.selection_hint: Select when the business touches a regulated domain (financial services, payments, health, insurance, food, alcohol, transport, crypto custody) or operates in jurisdictions with licensing gates before launch.

deliverable.artifact: licensing_roadmap
sections: Required licenses and registrations by jurisdiction
Gate sequence with dependencies and lead times
Owner, cost, and renewal cadence per gate

playbooks.rubric: Passes if every revenue-enabling activity is mapped to a specific named license or a documented exemption, each gate has an owner, a lead time, and a position in a dependency-ordered sequence.
